name: Reverse Leg Raises
description: Lay your stomach on a raised surface and raise your legs into a horizontal position

name: Planche Lean
description: Make sure your hands up at your waist position
steps: Start in a high push-up position with your hands shoulder-width apart and arms fully straight
Engage your core and glutes to keep your body in a straight line from head to heels
Push through the floor and protract your shoulders by rounding the upper back slightly
Slowly shift your shoulders forward past your wrists while keeping your elbows locked
Lean as far forward as you can while maintaining control and proper form
Keep your head neutral and look slightly ahead of your hands

name: Wall Sit
steps: Start by getting onto the floor on your hands and knees
Step your feet forward roughly 1–2 feet away from the wall
Slowly slide your back down the wall by bending your knees
Lower yourself until your thighs are parallel to the floor and your knees form roughly 90-degree angles
Keep your knees directly above your ankles and avoid letting them move inward
Press your back and shoulders firmly against the wall
Tighten your core and keep your chest upright
description: Sit in an imaginary chair pressing your backing into the wall. Your things should be parallel to the ground.

name: Plank
steps: Start by getting onto the floor on your hands and knees
Place your forearms on the ground with your elbows directly under your shoulders
Extend your legs straight behind you and balance on your toes
Keep your body in a straight line from your head to your heels
Tighten your core, glutes, and legs to prevent your hips from sagging or lifting too high
Keep your neck neutral by looking down at the floor slightly ahead of your hands

name: Diamond Push Up
steps: Start in a high push-up position with your hands placed close together beneath your chest
Position your thumbs and index fingers so they touch, forming a diamond shape.
Extend your legs behind you and balance on your toes.
Keep your body in a straight line from your head to your heels
Tighten your core and glutes to maintain stability
Slowly bend your elbows and lower your chest toward your hands
Keep your elbows close to your body as you descend
Lower yourself until your chest is just above your hands or as far as you can comfortably go
Push through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position
Keep your neck neutral by looking slightly ahead of your hands

name: Wide Push Up
steps: Start in a standard push-up position with your hands placed wider than shoulder-width apart
Keep your legs straight and balance on your toes with your body in a straight line.
Tighten your core and glutes to stabilize your body
Shift your weight to one side while bending that arm, keeping the opposite arm straight
Lower your chest toward the bent arm, as if doing a one-sided push-up
Keep the straight arm fully extended and use it for light support only
Push through the bent arm to return to the center position
Repeat the movement on the opposite side, alternating each rep
Keep your hips level and avoid twisting your torso too much

name: Archer Push Up
steps: Start in a standard push-up position with your hands placed wider than shoulder-width apart with hands pointing outward
Keep your legs straight and balance on your toes with your body in a straight line.
Tighten your core and glutes to stabilize your body
Shift your weight to one side while bending that arm, keeping the opposite arm straight
Lower your chest toward the bent arm, as if doing a one-sided push-up
Keep the straight arm fully extended and use it for light support only
Push through the bent arm to return to the center position
Repeat the movement on the opposite side, alternating each rep
Keep your hips level and avoid twisting your torso too much

progression.name: Bent Knee
progression.description: Perform the front lever without extending the legs. This reduces the lever and makes it easier to assume the position

progression.name: Straddle
progression.description: Perform the front lever with legs straddled apart to keep your centre of gravity closer to your hands
